subject: Chemistry
solution:
Hydrogen peroxide acts as an oxidising as well as reducing agent in both acidic and alkaline media.

But its reducing action is more effective in alkaline medium

KIO4+H2O2→KIO3+O2+H2O

The oxidation number of iodine in IO3- is +5

The geometry of iodine containing compound changes from tetrahedral to pyramidal in the above reaction

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Hydrogen undergoes the following changes:

1) While acting as an oxidising agent in an acidic medium,

H2O2+2H++2e-→2H2O

2) While acting as a reducing agent in an alkaline medium,

H2O2+2OH-→O2+2H2O+2e-

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The balanced chemical equation for the reaction of iron with steam is:

$3Fe + 4H_2O \rightarrow Fe_3O_4 + 4H_2$

Molar mass of $H_2O = 2 \times 1 + 16 = 18 \text{ g mol}^{-1}$

Number of moles of steam ($H_2O$) given = $\dfrac{18}{18} = 1 \text{ mol}$

From the balanced equation, $4 \text{ moles}$ of $H_2O$ react with $3 \text{ moles}$ of $Fe$.

Therefore, $1 \text{ mole}$ of $H_2O$ will react with $\dfrac{3}{4} \text{ moles}$ of $Fe$.

Mass of iron reacted = Moles of $Fe \times$ Molar mass of $Fe$

Mass of iron = $\dfrac{3}{4} \times 56 = 3 \times 14 = 42 \text{ g}$

Answer: $42$ g

solution:
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ acts as a reducing agent only in presence of strong oxidising agents (i.e., $\left.\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}\right)$in acidic as well as alkaline medium.
$$
\begin{aligned}
&2 \mathrm{KMnO}_4+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4+5 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow \\
&\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4+2 \mathrm{MnSO}_4+8 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+5 \mathrm{O}_2
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Chemistry
options: There is extensive intramolecular hydrogen bonding in the condensed phase.
Ice formed by heavy water sinks in normal water.
Water is oxidized to oxygen during photosynthesis
Water can act both as an acid and as a base
solution:
(i) Ice formed by heavy water sinks in normal water due to higher density of D2O than normal water.

(ii) 6 CO 2 + 6 H 2 O → chlorophyll hv C 6 H 12 O 6 +6O 2

(iii) Water can show amphiprotic nature and hence water can act both as an acid a base.

(iv) There is extensive intermolecular hydrogen bonding in the condensed phase instead of intramolecular H - bonding.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Molarity of H2O=Volume strength11.2

M=11.211.2=1

∴ 1 molar means 1000 mL solution contains 1 mol H2O2=1×34 g

Since, the strength is represented as weight of the solute in 1 L of the solution. The strength of the solution will be 34 g L-1.

Note: The question should've mentioned that we need to find the mass by volume percentage.

Percentage w/v =341000×100=3.4%

subject: Chemistry
options: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
A is correct but R is not correct
A is not correct but R is correct
Both A and R are correct but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
solution:
Loss of an electron from a hydrogen atom results in a nucleus of very small size as compared to other atoms or ions. Due to its small size it cannot exist free. Loss of the electron from hydrogen atom results in nucleus size (H+) of 1.5×10-3.

A proton is a positively charged subatomic particle that is present in the nucleus of an atom, and it can exist in various forms, including as a part of an atomic nucleus, as a free particle, or as a constituent of a larger particle such as a molecule. H+ always exists in combined form.

Both A and R are correct but there is no relation between two statements.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
During boiling MgHCO32 is converted to insoluble MgOH2

MgHCO32→BoilingMgOH2↓+2CO2

CaHCO32→boilingCaCO3↓+H2O+CO2

CaHCO32 changes to insoluble CaCO3

Mg(OH)2​ is less soluble than MgCO3. Hence, the products formed are CaCO3 and Mg(OH)2.​

subject: Chemistry
solution:
PbS+4H2O2→PbSO4+4H2O

In the above reaction lead sulphide oxidised to lead sulphate. Hence, hydrogen peroxide acts as oxidising agent.

2Fe2++H2O2→2Fe3++2OH-

In the above reaction Fe2+ oxidised to Fe3+. Hence, hydrogen peroxide acts as oxidising agent.

Mn2++H2O2→Mn4++2OH-

In the above reaction Mn2+ oxidised to Mn4+. Hence, hydrogen peroxide acts as oxidising agent.

HOCl+H2O2→H3O++Cl-1+O2

In the above reaction Cl+1 reduced to Cl-, hence, hydrogen peroxide acts as a reducing agent.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
As 1 L solution have 10-3 mol CaSO4

Eq.  of CaSO4=eq.  of CaCO3

In 1 L solution

nCaSO4×v.f=nCaCO3×v.f.

10-3×2=nCaCO3×2

nCaCO3=10-3 mol in 1 L

∴WCaCO3=100×10-3g   in 1 L solution

∴ hardness in terms of CaCO3

=WCaCO2Wtotal×106=100×10-3 g1000 g×106=100 ppm

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Tritium isotope of hydrogen is radioactive and emits low energy β- particles. It is because of high n/p ratio of tritium which makes nucleus unstable.

For tritium H13

No. of neutron n=2

No. of proton p=1

np=2

∵ np is high

tritium wil emit β particle.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In basic medium, oxidising action of H2O2.

Mn2++H2O2→Mn+4+2OH-

In basic medium, reducing action of H2O2

I2+H2O2+2OH-→2I-+2H2O+O2

In acidic medium, oxidising action of H2O2

PbS(s)+4H2O2(aq)→PbSO4( s)+4H2O(l)

Hence correct option (4).
